methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo is a pre-IPO artificial intelligence and software company founded by Sebastian Thrun and Anthony Levandowski. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of eight same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables in AI & Software, spanning one public company (Palantir at $400.0B) and seven private firms including OpenAI ($300.0B), xAI ($250.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), and Databricks ($134.0B).

A peer-median approach yields an implied valuation of $158.5B based on the median of the eight-company comp set, with the universe ranging from $29.3B to $400.0B. This method is applicable given the homogeneity of stage and sector positioning within the peer group. The target's current $126B valuation sits below the peer median by approximately $32.5B, consistent with pre-IPO pricing relative to public and late-stage private comparables.

The triangulated valuation range is $134.7B (low) to $182.3B (high), with a median of $158.5B. The current valuation of $126B falls modestly below this range. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of a potential public offering, which would materially shift comparable valuations, and the composition of the peer set itself, which concentrates on generative AI and data platforms rather than autonomous systems companies. A single-method analysis limits confidence; additional triangulation via revenue multiples or DCF would strengthen conviction.
